title: Benchmarks
text: benchmark summaries compare standard Giotto preprocessing workflow using GiottoDB dbMatrix-backed expression data -memory dgCMatrix file-backed H5SparseMatrix backends. Triangles denote runs reached 1,000 second timeout limit; x marks denote --memory (OOM) failures. workflow also benchmarked across full spatial technologies assess end--end runtime real input matrices.

title: Overview
text: GiottoDB module Giotto Suite ecosystem provides database support core Giotto functionality dbverse.

title: Overview
text: table summarizes GiottoDB-native methods Giotto functions supported GiottoDB objects.

title: Overview
text: Benchmark summaries available benchmarks vignette.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Edward C. Ruiz. Author, maintainer. Timur Rizvanov. Author. Jiaji George Chen. Author. Ruben Dries. Author, reviewer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ruiz E, Rizvanov T, George Chen J, Dries R (2026). GiottoDB: GiottoDB. R package version 0.99.0.
code: @Manual{,   title = {GiottoDB: GiottoDB},   author = {Edward C. Ruiz and Timur Rizvanov and Jiaji {George Chen} and Ruben Dries},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.99.0}, }

title: GiottoDB
text: goal GiottoDB provide dbverse interoperability objects Giotto Suite spatial omics data analysis R. Documentation available https://giotto-suite.github.io/GiottoDB/.

title: GiottoDB
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("giotto-suite/GiottoDB")

title: GiottoDB
code: library(GiottoDB) library(GiottoData) library(DBI) library(duckdb)  # Load a small example Giotto object. gobject <- GiottoData::loadGiottoMini("visium")  # Create GiottoDB object with a temporary local database. db_path <- tempfile("giottodb-mini-", fileext = ".duckdb") con <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), dbdir = db_path) gdb <- as_giottodb(gobject, con = con)  # Preview the GiottoDB object gdb DBI::dbListTables(con)  # Disconnect from the database and clean up DBI::dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)

title: GiottoDB Class — GiottoDB-class
text: S4 class extends giotto class provide database-backed implementation Giotto objects using dbMatrix::dbMatrix dbSpatial::dbSpatial.

title: GiottoDB Class — GiottoDB-class
text: GiottoDB object

title: GiottoDB Class — GiottoDB-class
text: GiottoDB class extends standard giotto class, replacing -memory objects database-backed alternatives appropriate: Expression matrices (matrix, Matrix) replaced dbMatrix::dbMatrix objects Spatial objects (points, polygons) replaced dbSpatial::dbSpatial objects allows Giotto scale larger--memory datasets maintaining API compatibility existing Giotto workflows.

title: GiottoDB Class — GiottoDB-class
text: conn DBIConnection object duckdb::duckdb database

title: GiottoDB Global Options — GiottoDB-options
text: GiottoDB sets specific global options optimize performance database-backed operations.

title: GiottoDB Global Options — GiottoDB-options
text: giotto.dbmatrix_compute (Logical) Controls whether normalizeGiotto automatically materializes result via compute() / .compute_dbMatrix(). Defaults TRUE GiottoDB prevent lazy query chain overhead downstream operations like db_svd. global option implemented Giotto package enabled default GiottoDB.

what: Create a new GiottoDB object — GiottoDB
title: Create a new GiottoDB object — GiottoDB
text: Create new GiottoDB object, database-backed implementation Giotto object.

title: Create a new GiottoDB object — GiottoDB
code: GiottoDB(con, ...)

title: Create a new GiottoDB object — GiottoDB
text: con DBIConnection object duckdb::duckdb database ... Additional arguments passed giotto constructor

title: Create a new GiottoDB object — GiottoDB
text: GiottoDB object

title: Create a new GiottoDB object — GiottoDB
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a new GiottoDB object with a new database connection library(GiottoDB) library(duckdb) library(DBI)  # Create a connection to a DuckDB database con <- dbConnect(duckdb(), dbdir = ":memory:") dbSpatial::loadSpatial(con) # Load spatial extension  # Create a new GiottoDB object gobj_db <- GiottoDB(con = con)  # Don't forget to close the connection when done dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE) } # }

what: Add statistics for Giotto / GiottoDB — addStatistics
title: Add statistics for Giotto / GiottoDB — addStatistics
text: S3 generic delegates Giotto::addStatistics() plain giotto objects, adds DuckDB-native polygon area support GiottoDB objects dbSpatial-backed polygons.

title: Add statistics for Giotto / GiottoDB — addStatistics
code: addStatistics(gobject, ...)  # S3 method for class 'giotto' addStatistics(   gobject,   feat_type = NULL,   spat_unit = NULL,   stats = c("feature", "cell", "area"),   expression_values = c("normalized", "scaled", "custom"),   detection_threshold = 0,   return_gobject = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'GiottoDB' addStatistics(   gobject,   feat_type = NULL,   spat_unit = NULL,   stats = c("feature", "cell", "area"),   expression_values = c("normalized", "scaled", "custom"),   detection_threshold = 0,   return_gobject = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )  # Default S3 method addStatistics(gobject, ...)

title: Add statistics for Giotto / GiottoDB — addStatistics
text: gobject giotto GiottoDB object. ... Additional arguments (currently ignored). feat_type Feature type. spat_unit Spatial unit. stats statistics compute. expression_values Expression values use. detection_threshold Detection threshold. return_gobject Whether return updated object. verbose Verbosity.

what: Convert GiottoDB Object to giotto — as_giotto
title: Convert GiottoDB Object to giotto — as_giotto
text: convenience function coerces GiottoDB object -memory giotto object. Expression matrices converted -memory matrices using dbverse coercions: dbSparseMatrix/dbMatrix -> dgCMatrix dbDenseMatrix -> base matrix Spatial data stored dbSpatial converted terra::SpatVector.

title: Convert GiottoDB Object to giotto — as_giotto
code: as_giotto(x, verbose = TRUE)

title: Convert GiottoDB Object to giotto — as_giotto
text: x GiottoDB object verbose Whether print progress messages

title: Convert GiottoDB Object to giotto — as_giotto
text: GiottoClass::giotto object

title: Convert GiottoDB Object to giotto — as_giotto
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(GiottoDB) library(GiottoData) library(duckdb) library(DBI)  g <- GiottoData::loadGiottoMini("visium") con <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), dbdir = ":memory:") gdb <- as_giottodb(g, con = con)  g_inmem <- as_giotto(gdb) DBI::dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE) } # }

what: Convert giotto Object to GiottoDB — as_giottodb
title: Convert giotto Object to GiottoDB — as_giottodb
text: convenience function coerces giotto object GiottoDB object database backend. Expression matrices converted dbMatrix objects spatial data converted dbSpatial objects.

title: Convert giotto Object to GiottoDB — as_giottodb
code: as_giottodb(   x,   con = NULL,   db_path = NULL,   prefix = "gdb_",   overwrite = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   temporary = TRUE )

title: Convert giotto Object to GiottoDB — as_giottodb
text: x giotto object con DBIConnection object duckdb::duckdb connection object db_path Path database file creating new persistent connection prefix string prefix add database table names overwrite Whether overwrite existing tables verbose Whether print progress messages temporary TRUE (default), create temporary table local connection automatically deleted con expires

title: Convert giotto Object to GiottoDB — as_giottodb
text: GiottoDB object

title: Convert giotto Object to GiottoDB — as_giottodb
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(GiottoDB) library(dbSpatial)  # Create connection con <- dbConnect(duckdb(), dbdir = ":memory:") loadSpatial(con)  # Convert Giotto object my_giotto_db <- as_giottodb(my_giotto, con = con)  # Don't forget to close the connection when done dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE) } # }

what: Calculate Highly Variable Features for GiottoDB — calculateHVF
title: Calculate Highly Variable Features for GiottoDB — calculateHVF
text: S3 method prevents accessing "scaled" expression avoid materialization.

title: Calculate Highly Variable Features for GiottoDB — calculateHVF
code: calculateHVF(gobject, ...)  # S3 method for class 'GiottoDB' calculateHVF(   gobject,   spat_unit = NULL,   feat_type = NULL,   expression_values = "normalized",   method = c("cov_groups", "cov_loess", "var_p_resid"),   reverse_log_scale = FALSE,   logbase = 2,   expression_threshold = 0,   nr_expression_groups = 20,   zscore_threshold = 1.5,   HVFname = "hvf",   difference_in_cov = 0.1,   var_threshold = 1.5,   var_number = NULL,   random_subset = NULL,   set_seed = TRUE,   seed_number = 1234,   show_plot = NULL,   return_plot = NULL,   save_plot = NULL,   save_param = list(),   default_save_name = "HVFplot",   return_gobject = TRUE,   calc_gini = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )  # S3 method for class 'giotto' calculateHVF(   gobject,   spat_unit = NULL,   feat_type = NULL,   expression_values = c("normalized", "scaled", "custom"),   method = c("cov_groups", "cov_loess", "var_p_resid"),   reverse_log_scale = FALSE,   logbase = 2,   expression_threshold = 0,   nr_expression_groups = 20,   zscore_threshold = 1.5,   HVFname = "hvf",   difference_in_cov = 0.1,   var_threshold = 1.5,   var_number = NULL,   random_subset = NULL,   set_seed = TRUE,   seed_number = 1234,   show_plot = NULL,   return_plot = NULL,   save_plot = NULL,   save_param = list(),   default_save_name = "HVFplot",   return_gobject = TRUE,   calc_gini = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Calculate Highly Variable Features for GiottoDB — calculateHVF
text: gobject giotto object spat_unit spatial unit feat_type feature type expression_values expression values use method method calculate highly variable features reverse_log_scale reverse log-scale expression values (default = FALSE) logbase reverse_log_scale TRUE, log base used? expression_threshold expression threshold consider gene detected nr_expression_groups (cov_groups) number expression groups cov_groups zscore_threshold (cov_groups) zscore select hvg cov_groups HVFname name highly variable features cell metadata difference_in_cov (cov_loess) minimum difference coefficient variance required var_threshold (var_p_resid) variance threshold features var_p_resid method var_number (var_p_resid) number top variance features var_p_resid method random_subset random subset perform HVF detection . Passing NULL runs HVF cells. set_seed logical. whether set seed random_subset used seed_number seed number use random_subset used show_plot show plot return_plot return ggplot object (overridden return_gobject) save_plot logical. directly save plot save_param list saving parameters GiottoVisuals::all_plots_save_function() default_save_name default save name saving, change, change save_name save_param return_gobject boolean: return giotto object (default = TRUE) calc_gini logical. Whether calculate Gini index feature. Set FALSE performance large datasets dbMatrix objects. verbose verbose

what: Calculate overlaps with dbSpatial-backed objects — calculateOverlap
title: Calculate overlaps with dbSpatial-backed objects — calculateOverlap
text: Compute overlaps dbSpatial-backed polygon point features keeping result database-backed.

title: Calculate overlaps with dbSpatial-backed objects — calculateOverlap
code: # S4 method for class 'dbSpatial,dbSpatial' calculateOverlap(   x,   y,   poly_subset_ids = NULL,   feat_subset_column = NULL,   feat_subset_values = NULL,   feat_count_column = NULL,   feat_subset_ids = NULL,   count_info_column = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Calculate overlaps with dbSpatial-backed objects — calculateOverlap
text: x Object spatial annotations: giottoPolygon, SpatVector polygons. Can also giotto object y Object features overlap: giottoPoints, giottoLargeImage, SpatVector points SpatRaster poly_subset_ids character vector. (optional) Specific poly_IDs use feat_subset_column character. (optional) feature info attribute subset feature points performing overlap calculation. feat_subset_values (optional) values matched feat_subset_column order subset feature points performing overlap calculation. feat_count_column character. (optional) column count information. Useful cases one detection reported per point. column called "count" present feature points data, automatically selected. feat_subset_ids deprecated. Use feat_subset_values instead. count_info_column deprecated. Use feat_count_column instead. verbose verbose ... additional params pass methods.

what: Count Matrix Processing for GiottoDB Objects — processData-dbMatrix-methods
title: Count Matrix Processing for GiottoDB Objects — processData-dbMatrix-methods
text: processData contains methods GiottoDB objects provide various normalization scaling operations count matrices implemented Giotto. Working methods delegate existing implementations Giotto: Library normalization (libraryNormParam) Log normalization (logNormParam) osmFISH normalization (osmFISHNormParam) Arcsinh transformation (arcsinhNormParam) L2 normalization (l2NormParam) Z-score scaling (zscoreScaleParam) Default normalization (defaultNormParam) - combines library + log List processing - enables composable operations Unsupported methods: TF-IDF normalization (tfidfNormParam) Quantile normalization (quantileNormParam) Pearson residuals normalization (pearsonResidNormParam) - requires dense intermediate matrices incompatible sparse backend Additionally, calculateHVF(expression_values = "scaled") normalizeGiotto(scale_feats = TRUE) supported GiottoDB objects. Feature centering handled implicitly PCA via db_svd.

title: Count Matrix Processing for GiottoDB Objects — processData-dbMatrix-methods
code: processData(x, param, ...)

title: Count Matrix Processing for GiottoDB Objects — processData-dbMatrix-methods
text: x dbMatrix object param S4 parameter class defining transform operation. Can : libraryNormParam - library size normalization logNormParam - log transformation osmFISHNormParam - osmFISH normalization arcsinhNormParam - arcsinh transformation l2NormParam - L2/Euclidean normalization zscoreScaleParam - z-score scaling defaultNormParam - default normalization (library + log) list - chained operations tfidfNormParam - supported quantileNormParam - supported pearsonResidNormParam - supported ... additional params pass underlying methods

title: Count Matrix Processing for GiottoDB Objects — processData-dbMatrix-methods

text: results lazily evaluated. Please read dbplyr::collapse.tbl_sql compute/save results db.

title: Count Matrix Processing for GiottoDB Objects — processData-dbMatrix-methods
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a dbMatrix object library(dbMatrix) con <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), ":memory:") mat <- matrix(rpois(100, 5), nrow = 10) dbmat <- dbMatrix(mat, con = con, name = "test")  # Library normalization lib_norm <- processData(dbmat, normParam("library"))  # Log normalization log_norm <- processData(dbmat, normParam("log"))  # Chained operations scaled <- processData(dbmat, list(   normParam("library"),   normParam("log"),   scaleParam("zscore") )) } # }

title: Tessellate a dbSpatial object — tessellate
code: coordinates <- data.frame(x = c(100, 200, 300), y = c(500, 600, 700)) attributes <- data.frame(id = 1:3, name = c("A", "B", "C"))  # Combine the coordinates and attributes dummy_data <- cbind(coordinates, attributes)  # Create a duckdb connection con = DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), ":memory:")  # Create a duckdb table with spatial points db_points = dbSpatial(conn = con,                       value = dummy_data,                       x_colName = "x",                       y_colName = "y",                       name = "foo",                       overwrite = TRUE) #> Error in dbSpatial(conn = con, value = dummy_data, x_colName = "x", y_colName = "y",     name = "foo", overwrite = TRUE): could not find function "dbSpatial"  tessellate(db_points, name = "my_tessellation", shape = "hexagon", shape_size = 60) #> Error in h(simpleError(msg, call)): error in evaluating the argument 'dbSpatial' in selecting a method for function 'tessellate': object 'db_points' not found

title: GiottoDB 0.99.0
text: Improve reconnection loading moving GiottoDB projects, including top-level database connections, cached connections, database lock-file handling. Fix Giotto-backed PCA HVF edge cases, including argument forwarding runPCA(), k = 1 PCA scaling, unsupported scaled-matrix selection. Improve -memory coercion as_giotto() safer placeholder handling saveGiotto(). Fix dbSpatial overlap outputs, including row column names, scalar matrix outputs, geometry validation, dynamic argument filtering calculateOverlap(). Resolve package installation errors caused regex escaping.

title: GiottoDB 0.99.0
text: Public release package! Add dbSpatial-backed methods spatial IDs, feature IDs, subsetting. Add findMarkers_one_vs_all() support GiottoDB objects. Support weighted overlapToMatrix() outputs dbSpatial objects. Support conversion giottoPoints spatial vectors dbSpatial objects as_giottodb().

title: GiottoDB 0.99.0
text: Remove stale generated vignette sources. Update pkgdown configuration site URL. Update package collate metadata imports.

title: GiottoDB 0.99.0

title: GiottoDB 0.99.0
text: Validate calculateHVF() residual variance behavior upstream Giotto changes. Add reconnection, conversion, overlap, PCA, HVF, full-pipeline test coverage. Update calculateOverlap() test expectations current behavior.
